title: hieracium maculatum subsp. divisum
intro: Hieracium maculatum subsp. divisum is a lesser-known subspecies of the spotted hawk's-beard plant, a type of flowering plant in the dandelion family. It has been traditionally used for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antiseptic properties, making it useful in treating wounds, infections, and skin conditions. The plant is believed to have various health benefits, including reducing inflammation, promoting wound healing, and supporting the immune system. Hieracium maculatum subsp. divisum can be prepared into herbal teas, infusions, or tinctures, although more research is needed to fully understand its potential uses and effects.
